title: Verification Report
description: Structured report emitted by the verifier (and rolled up by the synthesizer). Replaces uncalibrated 'X% sure' with a status enum and concrete evidence.

task_id.description: Identifier matching the Task Contract task_id.

status.description: VERIFIED: all gates pass, all AC PASS, no CRITICAL/HIGH finding, scope clean. PARTIALLY_VERIFIED: core passes, non-blocking residuals remain. BLOCKED: failed machine gate, CRITICAL finding, or unresolved contradiction. UNVERIFIED: insufficient evidence.

claim.description: The factual claim being supported (e.g., 'all unit tests pass on clean checkout').

observed_via.description: Exact command or action that produced the evidence (e.g., 'cd worktree-clean && pnpm test').

observed_output_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the observed tool output. Anchors the claim to a real run; never claim a result you did not observe.

pre_existing.description: True if this failure/behavior was already present at base_commit (pre-existing), false if newly caused.

evidence.description: Every claim in the report must be backed by an observed entry here.

ac_id.description: Matches an acceptance_criteria[].id from the Task Contract.

evidence_ref.description: Reference to the evidence[] entry supporting this result (e.g., claim string or index).

acceptance_results.description: One result per acceptance criterion from the Task Contract.

scope_violations.description: Empty if scope is clean. Any non-empty entry forces status != VERIFIED.

item.description: A concrete item that could not be verified (e.g., 'full integration suite not run — cost too high on this branch').

suggested_check.description: Cheapest discriminating check that would resolve the unknown.

residual_unknowns.description: Honest list of items not verified. Drives PARTIALLY_VERIFIED / UNVERIFIED.

forced_by_gate.description: If status != VERIFIED, the machine gate or finding that forced it (e.g., 'test_auth.py::test_race FAILED on clean checkout').
